intro.title: Hello, I'm Neomelt
intro.desc:
Robot development enthusiast, Junior Year Student.
Interning at a Humanoid Robot Company.
Focus on MCU & Linux development, Motion Control, RL, and Perception.
Email: neomelt@qq.com

item1.title: RoboMaster Sentry Robot Full-Stack Algorithm R&D
item1.desc: As algorithm team lead, I optimized sentry navigation, tracking, and decision pipelines. By tuning ROS2 Nav2 A* and MPPI modules, average planning time dropped by 30% and survivability improved by 20%+ in same-map benchmarks. I also deployed perception neural networks with ONNX and TensorRT for real-time inference acceleration.

item2.title: High-Fidelity Robot Simulation System Based on Gazebo Harmonic
item2.desc: To solve limited test-field access and high hardware wear, I led a ROS2 Jazzy + Gazebo Harmonic simulation platform. It includes IMU noise modeling, Livox Mid-360 LiDAR simulation, and URDF physics fitting with 90%+ Sim-to-Real logic coverage.

item3.title: PulseScope Cross-Platform Robot Debugging GUI
item3.desc: I independently developed a Rust/C++/Go/TypeScript debugging workstation with low-latency UDP/TCP transport and real-time visualization. After multi-process, memory-pool, and zero-copy optimization, memory usage dropped by 62% and end-to-end latency stabilized at <=28ms.

project1.title: RoboMaster Sentry Robot Full-Stack Algorithm R&D
project1.desc:
Since Sep 2024, I have led full-stack algorithm R&D for our RoboMaster sentry robot as algorithm lead. Our team won Third Prize at the RoboMaster 2025 Super Confrontation National Finals.

On navigation, I tuned ROS2 Nav2 Costmap Layer and A* parameters, reducing average global planning time per frame by 30%. On control, I optimized official Nav2 MPPI weights and constraints; in same-map benchmarks, tracking error P95 stayed within 5cm and survivability improved by 20%+.

I also built an EKF + OpenCV ROI dynamic prediction vision pipeline, achieving an 80%+ hit rate against targets spinning at 8rad/s. In addition, I delivered an inference deployment pipeline from trained checkpoints to ONNX export and TensorRT engine deployment for real-time battlefield perception.

In parallel, I led GitHub CI/CD and a unified Docker dev image, reducing new-member environment setup time to 30 minutes.

project2.title: High-Fidelity Robot Simulation System Based on Gazebo Harmonic
project2.desc:
From Jul 2025 to Sep 2025, I led this project to address limited physical testing space, high hardware wear, and poor reproducibility of extreme combat scenarios.

Built on ROS2 Jazzy and Gazebo Harmonic, the system includes IMU Gaussian noise modeling, Livox Mid-360 LiDAR point-cloud simulation, plus URDF inertia calibration and joint friction/collision parameter fitting.

The final platform achieved 90%+ Sim-to-Real logic verification coverage, significantly improving algorithm iteration speed and transfer efficiency from simulation to real robots.

project3.title: PulseScope Cross-Platform Robot Debugging GUI
project3.desc:
Since Oct 2025, I have been independently developing PulseScope, a cross-platform RoboMaster debugging workstation. The core is written in Rust, with a C++ communication module, a Go backend service, and a TypeScript frontend.

The system delivers low-latency end-to-end transport over UDP/TCP. I refactored the original single-thread architecture into multi-process isolation and applied memory-pool plus zero-copy optimization, reducing memory usage by 62% and keeping end-to-end latency stable at <=28ms.

PulseScope now supports real-time visualization for target states, filtering outputs, and control signals, plus hot parameter updates and one-click debug log export. It has been stably used in team field debugging and on-site emergency support.

item1.title: RGM (Rust GPU Monitor)
item1.desc: A real-time GPU monitoring tool for Linux, written in Rust. It currently supports NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, with planned Intel support.

item2.title: rm_sim_26 High-Fidelity Robot Simulation
item2.desc: A RoboMaster simulation project based on ROS2 Jazzy and Gazebo Harmonic, covering sensor noise modeling, URDF physics fitting, and Sim-to-Real validation.

item3.title: robomaster-monitor
item3.desc: A lightweight automation tool that tracks newly posted open-source projects on the RoboMaster forum and pushes real-time alerts to Lark.

item4.title: PulseScope Cross-Platform Debugging GUI
